title: What is true in this present moment?
subtitle: A journal set for separating present-moment reality from anxious projections at bedtime.
excerpt: A journal prompt set for Day 8 of the SLEEP program. Four prompts for distinguishing what is actually true right now from what the anxious mind is predicting, so the body can rest in what is real.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. Developed as a companion practice to Day 8 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

content.text: A journal set for Day 8 of the SLEEP program: finding the ground of what is actually true right now, as a counterweight to what anxiety is projecting. About fifteen minutes.
New to journal prompts as a practice? Read
What is the anxiety telling me will happen?
Write it plainly. What is the story, the worst case, the threat, the thing it is rehearsing? Get the loop out of the head and onto the page.
What is actually true right now, in this moment?
Not what might be true. Not what could be. What is factually, currently true, about your safety, your situation, this room, this night. Write only what you can verify.
Where is the gap between the two?
Name the distance between what anxiety is predicting and what is currently real. You don't need to dismiss the anxiety, just see the distance.
What is one thing that is good or neutral in this moment?
Not a gratitude list. One specific thing. Small counts.
Original to homemade.education. Developed as a companion practice to Day 8 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
